mission.title: istio: Any HTTP service will block all HTTPS/TCP traffic on the same port
mission.description: Any HTTP service will block all HTTPS/TCP traffic on the same port. Requested by 18+ users.

steps.title: Check current istio deployment
steps.description:
Verify your istio version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
helm list -n istio 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working istio installation.

steps.title: Review istio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant istio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get configmap -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=istio
```
(Assumes ALLOW_ANY mode)

Normally, traffic to external HTTPS services works due to the ALLOW_ANY mode changes we added. However, when an http service is added on port 443 (or any port, but generally this happens on 443), this breaks.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Any HTTP service will block all HTTPS/TCP traffic on the…
steps.description:
One alternative solution -- reject http Services on 443. Not sure if that is a worse evil than the bug though...

Or treat `http` on 443 as https or tcp (either one works). Both of these are pretty hacky
```yaml
curl https://www.google.com # works
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n istio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=istio
kubectl get events -n istio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Any HTTP service will block all HTTPS/TCP traffic on the…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
One alternative solution -- reject http Services on 443. Not sure if that is a worse evil than the bug though...

Or treat `http` on 443 as https or tcp (either one works). Both of these are pretty hacky
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kaf -
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata:
  name: https-breaker
spec:
  hosts:
  - wikipedia.org
  location: MESH_EXTERNAL
  ports:
  - number: 443
    name: http
    protocol: HTTP
  resolution: DNS
EOF

$ curl https://www.google.com
curl: (35) error:140770FC:SSL routines:SSL23_GET_SERVER_HELLO:unknown protocol.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with istio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
